mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or trigger fails to predict direction with accuracy > 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion that drives corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal shows Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows, or positive returns occur in < 50% of co-occurring increase instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when FEDFUNDS is rising, or 5-day returns are negative in > 55% of tightening-period INDPRO increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 205.0, inflationary pressures trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL crosses 205.0, or Rank IC is negative over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage growth that supports corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with PAYEMS growth.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth above 3.0% and monthly CPI increases above 2.5% create a stagflationary environment that forces monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Co-occurring high growth and high inflation periods yield positive 20d returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signal temporary supply chain bottlenecks that resolve quickly, allowing markets to price in rapid normalization and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of INDPRO decline instances during recovery regimes, or Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling windows.
